sanskrit: न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते । न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥
hindiTranslation: मनुष्य न तो कर्मोंका आरम्भ किये बिना निष्कर्मताको प्राप्त होता है और न कर्मोंके त्यागमात्रसे सिद्धिको ही प्राप्त होता है ॥
englishTranslation: A person does not reach actionless freedom by avoiding action, nor by renouncing action alone does one reach perfection.
essence: Freedom is not found by escaping action, but by changing your relation to it.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Freedom does not come from simply stopping action, and perfection does not come from merely giving things up. Real growth requires a deeper way of acting.
simpleInsight: You can stop running without becoming free. What matters is the inner way you act, not just whether you act at all.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna has asked for one clear answer. Krishna now corrects a false choice: neither doing nothing nor merely dropping responsibilities brings the highest state. The teaching must now move toward disciplined action without inner bondage.
modernRelevance: You quit the project, mute every notification, and hope the pressure disappears. It doesn’t. Relief comes when your relationship to action changes, not when action vanishes.
translationLiteral: न (not) / कर्मणाम् (of actions) / अनारम्भात् (from non-beginning) / नैष्कर्म्यम् (actionlessness) / पुरुषः (a person) / अश्नुते (attains) । न (not) / च (and) / संन्यसनात् (by renunciation) / एव (alone) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / समधिगच्छति (reaches)
